name: Ginger Beef with Bok Choy
recipeIngredient: 3/4 lb Boneless tender beef steak (sirloin, Rib eye or top loin)
1/2 lb Bok choy cabbage or romaine lettuce
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut beef across grain into thin slices.
2. In a medium bowl, combine 1 tablespoon each of lite soy sauce, cornstarch, and sherry with minced ginger and garlic. Stir in the beef. Let stand for 30 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, in another bowl, mix water, 2 teaspoons cornstarch, and remaining 2 tablespoons lite soy sauce.
4. Separate and rinse Bok choy; pat dry. Cut leaves crosswise into 1-inch strips, separating stems from leaves.
5. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a hot wok or large skillet over high heat.
6. Add beef and stir-fry for 1 minute; remove from pan.
7. Add remaining 1 tablespoon oil to the same pan. Add onion and slivered ginger; stir-fry for 2 minutes.
8. Add Bok choy stems; stir-fry for another minute longer.
9. Return beef and soy sauce mixture to the pan. Cook, stirring until the sauce boils and thickens.
10. Serve immediately. Serves: 4
